title: Greedy vs. Exhaustive Search
concept: Greedy search chooses only the top candidate at each step, risking bad paths; exhaustive search checks every possible sentence, which is computationally impossible.

exercises.prompt: We are going to learn how AIs search for the best words to write. What is the main trade-off we will look at?
exercises.explain: Greedy search looks at what is best immediately at the next step, while exhaustive search checks all possibilities.

cards.title: How AIs Choose Words
cards.body: When an AI writes a story, how does it choose the next word? Does it grab the quickest option, or does it plan out millions of combinations ahead of time? Let's explore the balance between speed and perfection.

cards.title: Greedy vs. Exhaustive Search
cards.body: In AI text generation, **Greedy Search** picks the absolute highest-probability word at each step and moves on. **Exhaustive Search** calculates the probability of every single possible sentence to find the absolute best one.

cards.title: The Foggy Mountain Climb
cards.body: Imagine you are climbing a mountain in a thick fog. A **greedy search** is like only looking at your feet and taking whatever step goes upward right now. An **exhaustive search** is like teleporting to check every single path to the top before taking a single step. One is fast but might trap you on a small hill, while the other takes forever!

cards.title: Predicting the Next Word
cards.body: If an AI starts a sentence with "The cat sat...", a **greedy search** immediately picks the most likely next word, like "on". An **exhaustive search** calculates the score of billions of sentences like "The cat sat on the mat", "The cat sat under the table", and "The cat sat lazily by the fire" before deciding.

cards.title: Is Exhaustive Always Better?
cards.body: You might think checking every option (exhaustive search) is always best because it's thorough. In reality, language has so many word combinations that an exhaustive search would take millions of years just to write a simple email!

cards.title: Comparing Search Strategies
cards.body: Let's compare how these two search strategies behave when generating text.

left.text: Fast and simple. Chooses the best word right now, but might get stuck in repetitive loops.

right.text: Slow and impossible. Tries to calculate every possible sentence combination in the universe.

exercises.prompt: Why don't we use exhaustive search for large language models in the real world?

options.text: It is super fast and runs easily on smartphones.
It is too slow because there are too many word combinations.
It only looks at one word at a time.

exercises.explain: Exhaustive search is theoretically perfect because it looks at every possible combination, but it is too slow to ever be used in real life.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Greedy search is computationally much faster than exhaustive search.
exercises.explain: Greedy search is very fast because it only evaluates one step at a time, rather than comparing combinations of steps.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to describe the fast but short-sighted search strategy.
exercises.sentence: A search strategy that only selects the single best next word without planning ahead is called a ________ search.
exercises.explain: A greedy search only looks at the immediate next step, making it very fast but prone to making mistakes down the line.

exercises.prompt: Which of these texts is a common failure of an AI using a simple greedy search?

options.text: A perfectly balanced, highly poetic story with complex grammar.
A sentence that has checked trillions of options and is perfectly optimized.
A repetitive sentence that gets stuck repeating the same words over and over.

exercises.explain: The output generated by Option C is the result of a greedy approach: it makes sense word-by-word at first but ends up in a repetitive loop because it didn't plan the sentence structure.

exercises.prompt: An AI model is generating a response using a greedy search strategy. Which of the following best describes how it makes decisions?

options.text: It checks every single combination of words to find the absolute best sentence.
It picks the single best word at each step, which is fast but can lead to mistakes.
It randomly selects words from a dictionary to generate creative sentences.
It is the slowest search method but guarantees perfect grammar every time.

exercises.explain: A greedy search immediately picks the absolute best single word at each step without looking ahead. This is lightning-fast, but it often misses better overall sentences because it gets stuck on early choices.
